video.title: Peter Sagan IMPOSSIBLE Sprint Through TINY SPACE | Volta a Catalunya Stage 6 2021

transcript.clean_text: welcome back to the lantern rouge youtube channel and to highlights of stage six the volta catalonia 2021 a day that could have been one for the break or one for the sprinters a hundred and ninety four kilometer stage from taragona to mataro again with over 2500 meters climbing that would make it difficult for the sprinters the first major climb wasn't particularly steep three cases six percent was the hardest section in the middle and then the last climb was two and a half k's at four and a half percent so it was definitely a day for the springers that we do have here in this race not the deepest field but we do have peters again so the big question was whether the teams interested in the sprint could control the inevitable breakaway attempts the early break had tahara to shan strakov skill moses janssen maharish once again he's been in so many breaks this week but borah hansgro and uae team emirates had this breakaway in control pretty much all day shelling pacing on the front for borah they were also assisted by dsm writing for max cantor their quick man and new a team emirates for sebastian milano the break still had a decent lead going into the moto circuit in barcelona but it was pretty clear that the sprinters teams wanted to have that break caught before the final climb which crests about 14k from the finished uae pacing here they knew that they wouldn't get any help from the gc teams or team ineos because there's nothing really to gain from this stage ineos were just hoping for a quieter day than the previous couple of stages struck off the gas prom attacked the break when the gap was at less than 30 seconds to the pelton the rest the break just accepted their fate and he would be caught with about 2.8 k's to go before that final climb all the sprinters were still in the group at this point segan was looking fine so he just needed to make it over this final short climb motherstar paced once again with cataldo on the climb presumably trying to drop peter sagan but the climb was neither long nor hard enough the only big move was james knox attacking over the top he got brought back very quickly by borrow hands grower remy cavania tried to counter marked by lucas hamilton on bike exchange who although he couldn't get across to his wheel cavania he was presumably trying to mark him for dion smith the quick man for bike exchange and you can see immediately the other teams interested in the sprint go to the front borah hansgroh and uae team emirates because that's the problem when you're named remy cavani he's obviously well known now as a breakaway specialist and the sprinter's teams were not willing to give him any leash the gap never went up above seven seconds to him despite him looking in good condition or shut him down asap even keldaman pacing with leonard camden to bring that back and so with no further moves it was going to be a sprint ineos pacing with rowan dennis on the front on the right hand side just keeping adam yates safe in case there's any crashes again with two lead out men in front of him in the last three k's daryl olympia with just one man in the middle of this group it's actually quick step on the left hand side who have the most riders cherny cavania etc and i think they were trying to set up a late move through one of these roundabouts that come with 700 and 500 meters to go but as we come to the last kilometer ineos have row and dennis on the front but he pulls off pretty shortly so pretty soon the quick step rider on the front realizes that he's got none of his teammates behind him he's going to pull off daryl olympia's third wheel with peter segan and milano fighting for his wheel fourth and fifth wheel behind and when israel startup nation's last lead outman pulls off there's going to be a lack of control which is going to allow attacks to come before that last roundabout paralympian sagan obviously aren't going to follow any moves or kick from this point and burn their sprint so it's actually lobato who was supposed to be leading out you'd think for soto who was on his wheel who lets his wheel go who attacks into this last roundabout but he's marked by remy cavania lobardo won the sterling stage in the tour down under in 2015 stage two a similar rolling course with not a deep sprinter's feel but cavani was having none of it perhaps this was the move cavania was hoping to have made and he's being chased down by max cantor eating wind and this is why positioning was crucial in this sprint canter had no lead out men he's been forced to go on the front and close down cavania so his sprint is pretty much going to be toast he's got van rensburg impe sagan soto then molano behind him with 250 meters to go and daryl olympia is going to kick pretty early with 180 meters to go on a slightly uphill finish again initially trying to follow his wheel and then just squeezes before valerensburg and max cantor and once he hit clear air peters again it was clear that he was the best sprinter in this group and he's been doing this for over a decade again he doesn't rely on a lead out man you see jordy maeus on the right-hand side he blew up with about 300 meters to go sagan didn't try and follow him sagan just wanted to be in the top five wheels going into the last 300 meters and he'd allow his legs and positioning to do the rest whereas milano he picks the wrong wheel sega was on impe a good will to follow milano is on soto's wheel the orange just catella rider sixth wheel and he's going to lose segan's wheel with 180 meters to go when segan kicks to follow daryl impe that means milano has to come around him and i think melania was the only man at this race who might have been quick enough to beat peter sagan but his positioning wasn't good enough the only way segan could have lost this sprint is if that gap closed between van rensburg and canter but in vintage segan fashion through the eye of the needle he goes and jumps away from daryl olympia he'd already been sprinting for 100 meters at this point and borah has grown have to be so happy with how this catalonia has gone kelderman on gc has performed way above expectations given the severity of his crash just recently kemna taking a big stage win yesterday and now peter sagan he wasn't even supposed to be here covert derailed his preparation so he came to catalonia to get some more racing in the legs and he takes his first bunch sprint victory in nearly two years the last one being in stage five of the tour de france ahead of me trenton and van avermaet in 2019 in the green jersey but let's hear what he had to say after the stage well a little bit yeah we plan to control the race from the start and i have to say thanks to all my teammates boros grower and everyone that they controlled the race from the start until the end and it was very fast and great race today thank you yeah well it was pretty hard from the start yeah after tirano i'm in san remo without some longer break to come here i'm a little bit tired now but still i'm very happy for this victory it's a good start for a season and also especially after what i passed last uh two months before game here it was not easy and uh yeah now it looks nice let's have a look at the final top 10 on this stage peter sagan obviously taking out the stage daryl empty second smashing milano third van rensburg fourth camp fentorini canter almeida valgrind and van giels making up the top ten no movement on gc today an easier day for ineos adam yates still leading gc over richie port and grant thomas by 45 and 49 seconds respectively making up the ineos one two three on the podium stage seven the last stage of this race is the traditional barcelona circuit 132.5 k's and they do five laps of an 8k circuit that includes this nasty little climb around the mondewith castle and this stage has to be all about mother star and alejandra valverde number one congratulating segan marvel star tried a few things in this race they dropped the game in stage one but they didn't get a result there it's their home race mark solaire and enric mass have been a little bit disappointing so this stage seven has to be all about alejandra valverde he's 14 seconds behind guarant thomas on gc he's won this stage before back in 2017. mother star have to ride a hundred percent for the valverde stage win today to take the ten bonus seconds and hopefully gap thomas so they can also get valverde on the gc podium but otherwise i'll see with the highlights of stage seven the last stage of this very entertaining catalonia tomorrow ciao you
